steps.title: Identify what the goal requires
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize the variety of soundscapes. To accomplish this, the answer must show multiple different types of sounds together in one sentence — not focus narrowly on a single soundscape. Any choice that highlights only one soundscape fails the goal.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The sound of water flowing over Nachi Falls was selected for its cultural significance to Japan.': This describes only one soundscape (Nachi Falls) — it does not convey variety. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The Japanese Ministry of the Environment included the sound of cicadas at the Yama-dera temple on its list of 100 soundscapes of Japan.': This describes only one soundscape (cicadas at Yama-dera) — it does not convey variety. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The sound of birds at the Higashiyama Botanical Garden is on the Japanese Ministry of the Environment's list.': This describes only one soundscape (birds at Higashiyama) — it does not convey variety. ✗
  • Choice D — 'A range of different soundscapes are on the list, including the sounds of cicadas, birds, and water.': This explicitly names "a range of different soundscapes" and lists three distinct examples, directly emphasizing variety. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
